---
title: "Logistic Regression with missing values"
author: "Pierre Guillaumont et Stéphane Sadio"
date: "24/11/2021"
output: html_document
---

```{r library}
library(tictoc)
library(misaem)
library(caret)
library(ROCR)
```

```{r init}
n <- 100  # number of subjects
p <- 3     # number of explanatory variables
mu.star <- 1:p  # mean of the explanatory variables
sd <- 1:p # standard deviations
# with correlation
#C <- matrix(c(   # correlation matrix
#  1,   0.8, 0,   0,   0,
#  0.8, 1,   0,   0,   0,
#  0,   0,   1,   0.3, 0.6,
#  0,   0,   0.3, 1,   0.7,
#  0,   0,   0.6, 0.7, 1), nrow = p)
C = matrix(c(1, 0.8,0, 0.8, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1), nrow = p)
Sigma.star <- diag(sd) %*% C %*% diag(sd) # variance-covariance matrix of the explanatory variables
# beta.star <- c(0.5, -0.3, 1, 0, -0.6) # coefficients of logistic regression
beta.star <- c(0.5, -0.3, 1)
beta0.star <- -0.2  # intercept
beta.true = c(beta0.star,beta.star)

#percentage of missingness
p.miss <- 0.20 
tau = seq(0.5, 1, by = 0.1)
nbsim = 10
predSAEM = predNAall = predCCall = NULL
y.confusion = y.confusionSAEM = NULL
pAll = pAllSAEM = NULL
SAEM_tau = matrix(0,nrow = 6, ncol = 4)
Tau = seq(0.5,1, 0.1)

EST.small.saem = EST.small.comp = EST.small.cc  = matrix(0, nbsim,length(beta.star)+1)
TIME.small.saem  = rep(0, nbsim)
STD.small.saem = STD.small.comp = STD.small.cc = STD.small.mice = matrix(0, nbsim,length(beta.star)+1)
LENGTH.small.saem = LENGTH.small.comp = LENGTH.small.cc = matrix(0, nbsim,length(beta.star)+1)
count.small.saem = count.small.comp = count.small.cc = rep(0,p+1)
```



```{r SAEM, warning = FALSE}
tic()

for (NB in 1:nbsim)
{
  set.seed(NB)
  # complete data simulation
  X.small.complete <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n) %*% chol(Sigma.star) + matrix(rep(mu.star,n), nrow=n, byrow = TRUE)
  X.test = matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n) %*% chol(Sigma.star) + matrix(rep(mu.star,n), nrow = n, byrow = TRUE)
  p1 <- 1 / (1 + exp(-X.small.complete %*% beta.star - beta0.star))
  pAll = c(pAll, p1)
  y.test = as.numeric(runif(n) < p1)
  
  # ----- No NA : classical estimation in the case without missingness
  data.small.complete <- data.frame(y = y.test, X.small.complete)
  model.small.complete <- glm(y.test~. , family = binomial(link = 'logit'), data = data.small.complete)
  beta0.small.complete <- model.small.complete$coefficients[1]
  beta.small.complete <- model.small.complete$coefficients[2:(p + 1)]
  P.no.NA <- predict(model.small.complete, type = "response")
  predNA = P.no.NA
  predNAall = c(predNAall, predNA)
  
  # generating missing data - MCAR missingness
  X.obs <- X.small.complete
  patterns = runif(n * p) < p.miss
  X.obs[patterns] <- NA
  
  # ------- CC : estimation ignoring the missing data
  data.obs <- data.frame(y=y.test, X.obs)
  model.obs <- glm(y.test~. , family = binomial(link = 'logit'), data = data.obs)
  beta0.small.cc <- model.obs$coefficients[1]
  beta.small.cc <- model.obs$coefficients[2:(p+1)]
  ###
  X.obs[patterns] = 0
  data.obs2 <- matrix(X.obs, ncol = 3)
  P.CC = data.obs2 %*% beta.small.cc + beta0.small.cc
  ###
  predCC = P.CC  
  predCCall = c(predCCall,predCC)
  y.confusion = c(y.confusion, y.test)
  
  # -------  SAEM 
  
  k = 1 
  for (tau in seq(0.5,1, 0.1)) 
  {
    list.small.saem = miss.saem(X.obs, y.test, print_iter = FALSE, var_cal = TRUE, tau = tau)
    beta.small.saem = list.small.saem$beta
    std.small.saem = list.small.saem$std_obs
    
    pred = pred_saem(X.test, list.small.saem$beta, list.small.saem$mu, list.small.saem$sig2)
    predSAEM = c(predSAEM,pred) 
    pAllSAEM = c(pAllSAEM, p1)
    y.confusionSAEM = c(y.confusionSAEM, y.test)
    
    SAEM_tau[k,] = SAEM_tau[k,] + c(confusionMatrix(table(round(pred),y.test))$table) #confusion matrix in vector
    k = k + 1 
  }

}
```

```{r plot, echo = FALSE}
for (i in 1:6) 
{
  A = data.frame(matrix(c(SAEM_tau[i,]),2,2),row.names = c("0","1"))
  colnames(A) = c("0", "1")
  print(c("matrice de confusion pour tau = ",Tau[i]))
  print(A/sum(A))
}
print("matrice de confusion avec le modèle complet")
print(prop.table(confusionMatrix(table(as.integer(predNAall > 0.5), y.confusion))$table))
print("matrice de confusion sans se soucier des valeurs manquantes")
print(prop.table(confusionMatrix(table(as.integer(predCCall > 0.5), y.confusion))$table))

toc()
```


```{r ROC NA}
pre = prediction(pAll, round(predNAall)) # true model impossible to get
prf <- performance(pre, measure = "tpr", x.measure = "fpr")
plot(prf, colorize = FALSE, main = "ROC on validation set - NA ")
abline(a=0, b= 1, col="grey80")

```

```{r ROC SAEM}
pre = prediction(pAllSAEM,round(predSAEM))
prf <- performance(pre, measure = "tpr", x.measure = "fpr")
plot(prf, colorize = FALSE, main = "ROC on validation set - SAEM ")
abline(a=0, b= 1, col="grey80")

```

```{r ROC CC}
pre = prediction(pAll,as.integer(predCCall > 0.5))
prf <- performance(pre, measure = "tpr", x.measure = "fpr")
plot(prf, colorize = FALSE, main = "ROC on validation set - CC ")
abline(a=0, b= 1, col="grey80")

```
